text: How to make a Game?
This is such a question
That you can find in the comment section of every videos
And any new viewer who visits the channel
asks the same question
Are you also a new viewer?
If yes , then listen SUBSCRIBE the channel right now or I will shoot you
So yeah this video is on the topic that how can you make games on your own
And whatever doubts you have , related to Game Development
I will clear those doubts too
Like - Can we make games on Mobile?
What are the system requirements required to make Games
How can we make Games without Coding
Does making Game requires Money?
And many more!
But watch this video full
Because if u miss anything , then later you will say -
Vab brother I have made the script of the game
But where should I put it?
There is only Google here
IDIOT!
Okay lets start How to make a Game
So to make a Game , firstly you will need a Game Engine
Where we make Games.....Obviously
and you will find many Game Engines in the market where you can make Games
But I will not cover those Engines
Because I don't know which ones are they
So I will say about those Engines which I personally know
And comparing these Engines , I will also say that which one is the best for you
Because many people wants to make Games without Coding
YOU BACK BENCHERS!
So the Engines I know are -
Unreal Engine, Unity and Godot
Yeah , I know these 3 Engines only
So firstly let us see How we can make games in Unreal
And yeah , this is the same Engine which I have mentioned in the last 2 videos
And yeah, again this the same Engine which is used to make triple A Games
For example , Tomb Raider
The Witcher
And your popular Battle Royale Game - PUBG
And those who thinks that this Engine is Paid
Then NO, this Engine is completely free
You don't have to pay them in the starting
Until your Game earns more than 1 million dollars in the market
But if it earns more than 1 million dollars
Then you have to pay them 5% revenue as Royalty Fee
But don't focus on this now as its way too far
Lets see how Games are being made here!
So to make Games here , you will have 2 options for Coding
Visual Scripting and Actual Coding
And in their Actual Coding , they use the world's hardest language
Yes , C++
And for this reason , I didn't chose Unreal Engine
But its alternate is very easy , that is Visual Scripting
Also known as BLUEPRINTS
Here you can make Games without Coding
And by just joining nodes with each other
For example , if you have seen Choo Choo Charles
Then that game is made completely in BLUEPRINTS
So yeah compared to C++ , BLUEPRINTS are more easy
But I cant say that it is more easy than C#
And yeah I am telling now only
That Unreal is not beginner-friendly
Because its user interface is more suited to professional people
And yeah there's one things that I like about Unreal
That is , in their Marketplace , there are many High Quality Assets
That you will find FREE
Now you will ask that , "What's new in this?"
"Every Engine has to give it"
I AGREE
But they have given so much High Quality Assets in FREE
That while making your Game , you will need nothing
Here you can find Player ,
Environment ,
3D Models,
Animations,
Scripts too
And even Brain
THAT YOU DONT HAVE
Just you have to do some Coding and nothing else!
Which means BLUEPRINTS
Yeaaaahhhhhhh
Now Lets see How Games are being made in Unity
And for those who don't know where I make Games
THEN SHAME ON YOU!
I have said 1000 times in Unity
Hello Guys , now also I am making a new project in Unity
Hello Guys , now also I am making Games in Unity
Hello FOOLS , now I am harassing the player
Yeaaaahhhhh
So Go Away
~ohh godd~
And those who don't understand this
Then LISTEN
The word that is written big on my back
That is written for people like you only
IDIOT people
So yeah I am saying again , that I make Games in Unity only
And if you see the revenue share of Unity
Then you will see that Unity doesn't charges any royalty fee
But if your Game earns more than 100k dollars
Then you have to buy Unity Pro of Unity for which you have to pay every year
FORCEFULLY
which means that it will cut itself from earnings only
But yeah its way too far
Apart from that , Unity is basically beginner-friendly
Even Unity has its own tutorials!
To teach the new users about how to make Games
And Unity has also 2 options for Coding
1st , Real Coding and 2nd , Visual Scripting that is BOLT
And for coding , we use C# language here
Which is a very easy language to learn
And this is even a popular language for Game Development
And their Documentation is also easy to learn
By which you can learn this Engine very well
And they have their own Asset Store where you can find many Game Assets
FREE and PAID both
Yeah , PAID Assets are only more
But no worries , will tolerate
And for their 2nd Coding option
You can use their Visual Scripting that is BOLT
They are also like BLUEPRINTS of Unreal
But a little buggy
Till now , I have not seen any popular game that is made with BOLT
So yeah I will not recommend you to use BOLT
But , Its Your Choice
But yeah Unity is good for making
2D and 3D Games
Now only one Engine is left
GODOT
Lets see , What is GODOT?
So see , GODOT is basically a 2D Game Engine
And this Engine is full Open Source
Which means no matter how much money you make from this Engine
GO , EVERYTHING'S YOURS
This was a good one what he said
And as a Programming Language , it supports many languages
Like C# , C++ , Python , Java Script and many other languages
That probably I don't know
And they have also started to introduce 3D workspace
By which you can make 3D game too
But I will not recommend you to use this from now only
Because till now , it has not been implemented well
Which means that it is still in Development Phase
So you may face some problems
And their Documentation Support is better than both Unity and Unreal
So it's Noice!
So I have explained all the Engines
Now , let's see which Engine will be best for you
So see , if you are a complete beginner
And you don't even know about Hello and Bye World in Coding
Then I will suggest you Unity
Because you can use C# here to quickly learn to make Games
And the user interface of Unity is very beginner-friendly
So your learning speed will increase more
And I also make Games in Unity
So I will say in a while that how you can learn to make Games like me
But now , if you want to make Games with Coding with 0 knowledge of it
Then I will suggest Unity only
And in Unity , you can make both 2D and 3D Games
Which is a very nice thing!
Because it will affect the rest Engines
So if you are more  lazy than me
And don't want to do Coding
Then you can choose Unreal Engine
Which BLUEPRINTS!
But yeah , with this you will be able to make only 3D Games
Because their 2D Engine is very bad
But if you want to make only 2D Games
Then I will recommend both Unity and Godot
Because both Engines are good about this
And yeah this video is not Sponsored
I am just sharing my own experience
And I am suggesting Unity because
You can try both 2D and 3D Games here
And C# is used here which is very easy to learn
And if you learn this language , then the rest languages also becomes easy to learn
Like C++ which comes under C and even C# also comes under C
And if you don't know about C , then it is also a language only
And C# and C++ are under this language only
And if you want to use Unreal and Godot
Then you can also switch it from Unity
And you will have experience , so it will be easy to switch
Its up to you Guys , Select it on your own
I am just saying the easy way
System Requirements
See , the system requirements for Unity and Unreal are also same
Minimum requirements you will need is 8GB ram
The more , the better
And you can run both the software in 4GB ram
But the chances of crashes are more
Don't even try in 2GB ram
You Know , It will Blast
Just Kidding
Here Operating System Windows7 and + will run
And make sure that the threads of your CPU is more than 6
And according to them , your GPU VRAM should be at least 6GB
And if you want to know in detail
I have provided the link of the System Requirements in the description
You can checkout from there
And the System Requirements for Godot is very low
Here , you can make games even in 4GB ram!
Which is Nice
And the rest of its requirements almost matches with Unity and Unreal
So I will also provide the link of it in the description
Vab brother , Mobile!
Yeah coming to Mobile also in a while , WAIT
So Hardware is done , Now let's talk about how to learn to make Games
So see , I don't have proper idea for Unreal and Godot that how u can learn it
But I have the shared the Beginner Guide in Discord
From here , you can follow these tutorials and learn to make Games
And many people think that Game is made like this -
Hey Listen ,
If I press A , then move forward
If I press S , then move right
If I press D , then move to the south
And if I press W , then go to hell
NO FOOL , NO OFFENSE
So to make a proper Game , you will need 4 things -
Game Design , Programming , Game Engine and Art
Lets side the Game Design because you will learn it time to time while making Games
Here basically this happens that what and how things are gonna work in the Game
Ignore this now because as a beginner , I did the same
And let us side the Art too because we will see it later
So the remaining are Game Engine and Programming
So now let's talk about Unity
And to be honest , I can't teach you to make games myself
Because it will take a lot of time for me to teach you
Because as you know , I have other projects also to complete
But the place from where I learnt , you can learn from there too!
And that is BRACKEYS
From this channel , you will learn more than half of Game Development
And as a beginner , I followed this series of Brackeys fully
For which I was able to learn Game Development
And I will also recommend you to watch this series
This man teaches very well
Yeah , his channel is dead now but its enough for you guys
And yeah I have provided the link of the important tutorials in my Discord Server
From there , you can learn with 0 Coding knowledge
And yeah , for making Games I have not done any Paid Courses
Because everything is available in the Internet
That too in FREE
And I will also not recommend you to take up Paid Courses in the starting
Because many people pullout from half only
I am not saying that don't do course
Do course , even Advance course But after learning Unity
And I don't think there's necessary to even take
Because Advance tutorial courses are already available in Youtube
And for you , I have also opened a Tutorials Channel
Where I will try to teach you by making Advance tutorials
And yeah from the Internet , you can only learn 50% of Game Making
The rest 50% , you have to learn from your own even also if you take Paid Courses
Now , ART
Mainly 3D Models , Textures and Audio comes under this
And no need to learn this in starting only
You can learn this later too
And to dodge this , you can use third party resources in starting
Like many things can be found in Asset store
And by this , your learning will also become fast!
And I have also given Blender and Photoshop tutorials in Discord
From there , you can learn these too
Blender is basically used for 3D modelling
And Photoshop for 2D and for textures
MOBILE GAME DEVELOPMENT
See , to be honest , you cannot make a proper game using mobile
Because.... It is not practical only
Your Anroid Device will blast
But still if you want to try
Then there's an application in Playstore called Magic Engine
That you can try for learning purpose
And in Discord , we already have a Game Development Community
If you face any problem while making Games , you can ask here
Someone will surely help you and if I am available , then I myself will help you
And main thing
if you need the logics of how proper Games are made
Then start watching Devlogs
I also post Devlogs but they are very small
But next I am making a Multiplayer Game
So I will start making proper Devlogs of it
By which , you will be able to learn a lot!
So that's it for this video!
I don't know if there's anything I have missed
The rest say in the comments that how will you start making your Game
AND BYE.
